purpose: The commit fixes a bug where Bluetooth commands were queued after device unregistration, preventing potential use-after-free issues.
The commit removes an unnecessary header include to clean up code structure.
The commit updates documentation for a new 'N/A' store metric in perf c2c, reflecting changes in the report table.

The commit fixes race conditions and lock handling issues in the hugetlb subsystem, ensuring proper vma lock management during split and unmapping. It addresses existing bugs and prevents future ones by improving lock allocation and freeing mechanisms.
The commit adds documentation for AVDD and MICVDD power supplies in the Realtek rt5682s codec device tree bindings.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the remove function by adding a missing iounmap() call.
The commit removes an unused 'work' field from a struct in the mlx5e driver, which is part of code cleanup.

The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed to avoid static analysis warnings. The change is made because assigning -1 when the key is NULL could cause warnings, and the valid range needed is -1..3.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs without any functional changes.
The commit introduces a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() and replaces an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode configuration in the FIFOs.
The commit modifies self-tests to skip when RTC hardware isn't present, preventing unnecessary test failures.
Removes a redundant assignment to variable 'bytes' in ALSA echoaudio code, addressing a dead store warning without changing functionality.
The commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks to reduce kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes duplicated text 'be split' in spte.h, improving code readability and maintainability.
The commit reverts a previous change that caused a bug related to the clock table population based on dcfclk states. This was done to prevent a 'no read situation' when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit adds documentation for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU by including a compatible string in the device tree bindings.
The commit renames a struct to follow naming conventions for consistency.
The commit fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting the format specifier in a debug message to properly handle integer promotions.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS jump_label by adjusting the branch range check to avoid negative offset warnings.
The commit removes a specific case from SetHwReg8188EU() function by directly calling another function, aiming to eliminate an unwanted HAL layer as part of code cleanup.
The commit changes non-global symbols to static in response to sparse warnings, improving code quality and preventing potential bugs.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from device tree bindings for MDSS nodes, improving code accuracy and maintainability.
The commit allocates arrays in struct ctrl_ref to allow drivers to change array sizes, improving memory management and preparing for future driver needs.
The commit adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs to the socinfo driver.
The commit updates the code to recognize all possible chip cuts (versions) from A (0) to P (15), improving the handling of different chip versions.
The commit removes a custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks on ux500, relying instead on the core's existing mechanism. This simplifies the code by eliminating redundant state tracking.
The commit aims to reduce the number of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL directives by organizing and grouping related code sections, improving code readability and maintainability.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree configuration to allow adding functioning I2C nodes later.
The commit improves code readability by fixing comments, correcting typos, and adjusting coding style.

The commit fixes an infinite loop that occurs when failing to acquire a hardware semaphore during resource release, typically due to sudden hardware removal. It adds error handling to log the issue and exit gracefully.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI terminology for clocking, improving code quality.
The commit removes an unnecessary code check (IS_SWAPFILE) that was always true, thus cleaning up redundant code.
The commit adds functionality to handle GTP Echo Requests by sending appropriate responses, ensuring compliance with GTP specifications.
The commit refactors code by replacing cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate for efficiency. It also introduces cpumask_first_zero() to handle cases starting from zero.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver, which was causing console errors during STA roaming due to unsupported firmware functionality.
The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier by creating a new board configuration and connecting components accordingly.

The commit modifies the handling of zero-copy sendmsg operations to fail when the underlying socket does not support them. This prevents potential bugs by ensuring unsupported operations are properly handled.
The commit modifies the documentation build process to check for xeCJK.sty before loading it, preventing xelatex errors on systems where texlive-xecjk is not properly installed.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to stop reporting errors for certain macros used in kernel drivers, allowing these 'case' macros without issues.

The commit refactors the simpledrm driver by replacing its KMS helpers with atomic helpers to improve code architecture and enable better code sharing among drivers. No functional changes are introduced.
The commit adds a jpeg decoding worker interface to enable three hardware components to run in parallel on MT8195.
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls in libata as the information is covered by tracepoints, improving code quality.
The commit adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods in AMD display drivers to optimize power usage.

The commit fixes a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure caused by a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback's reset_type parameter. It corrects the function pointer prototype to use 'enum kfd_preempt_type' instead of 'uint32_t', resolving the CFI violation.
The commit corrects a typo in the device tree binding property name from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds validation to prevent future issues.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by adding a check to prevent accessing beyond the array size, thus improving code robustness.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver to simplify the code.
The commit moves fixed file installation and removal functions to the filetable code area and modifies function parameters for better design.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder nodes, enabling independent control through a power-domain controller. This allows each block to be managed separately.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro as it was only used once within another macro, simplifying the code.
